homePage.title: Gathering people who help others connect with nature.
homePage.mission: We gather to cultivate depth through community building, engage broadly through networking, and steward care for life by generously sharing skills, knowledge, and lived experience.
howItWorks: Seasonal gatherings convene members around practical themes and facilitated connection.
Unconference-style sessions create room for peer learning, collaboration, and emergent topics.
Members contribute resources, referrals, and lessons learned from real-world practice.

highlightCards.title: For practitioners and educators
highlightCards.description: A professional community for people weaving nature connection into their work.

highlightCards.title: Rooted in practice
highlightCards.description: Tools, stories, and methods are shared in plain language and grounded in experience.

highlightCards.title: Participation first
highlightCards.description: Join events, contribute ideas, and help shape the direction of the community.

pages.title: About Nature Connected Professionals
pages.intro: Nature Connected Professionals is a member-powered network supporting people who use nature connection in counseling, education, health, and community practice.
pages.heroImageAlt: Hero image for About Nature Connected Professionals

events.title: Fall Gathering: NCP Unconference 2026
events.summary: The NCP Fall *Un*conference will begin with an evening of camping, followed by a day filled with meaningful and creative experiences to build community.
events.details:
The NCP Fall *Un*conference will begin on Friday, October 2nd, with an evening of camping. Presentations, workshops, facilitated conversations, and lightning talks will be held on Saturday, October 3rd, from 9:00 am to 5:00 pm. More details and sign up to follow!

__Theme:__ *Building Intentional Community in Relationship with Nature.*

__The guiding roots for our growth:__ *Reciprocity, Connection, & Justice*

We have created three containers for engagement.

__Plot A:__ The Great Tree - Offering ideas and practices on a topic through a presentation or workshop. (Submission requested for planning purposes.)

__Plot B:__ Campfire Conversations - Facilitating a shared conversation about a topic without a specific agenda. (Submission requested for planning purposes.)

__Plot C:__ Community Cafe Lightening Talks - Spontaneous sparks that must be shared. (No submission necessary.)

This is a co-creative process. If you’re not sure it’s going to work, it’s OK. Submit your idea, and we’ll talk to you.

events.title: Summer Gathering 2026
events.details: __Call for Facilitators!__ If you have a nature-connecting experience that you'd like to test out or offer to an open, warm, and enthusiastic group of nature-loving professionals, please email Becca Manthe at becca@intuitivetherapyclinic.com. Facilitators are supported by the NCP planning committee. Unsure? Reach out, and we'll chat!

events.title: Spring Gathering 2026
events.location: [Boom Island Park](https://www.google.com/maps/place/724+Sibley+St+NE,+Minneapolis,+MN+55413/@44.9927359,-93.2692605,16z/data=!3m1!4b1!4m6!3m5!1s0x52b3327db5d698c7:0xaa11c30f709b8269!8m2!3d44.9927359!4d-93.2692605!16s%2Fg%2F11c43y1dg1?entry=ttu&g_ep=EgoyMDI2MDQxNS4wIKXMDSoASAFQAw%3D%3D) (entrance/parking furthest north, near picnic pavilions and Paddle Bridge Kayak Tours - 724 Sibley St NE, Minneapolis, MN 55413
events.summary: We'll gather by the water for a stewardship river clean up and networking while we work at [Boom Island Park](https://www.google.com/maps/place/724+Sibley+St+NE,+Minneapolis,+MN+55413/@44.9927359,-93.2692605,16z/data=!3m1!4b1!4m6!3m5!1s0x52b3327db5d698c7:0xaa11c30f709b8269!8m2!3d44.9927359!4d-93.2692605!16s%2Fg%2F11c43y1dg1?entry=ttu&g_ep=EgoyMDI2MDQxNS4wIKXMDSoASAFQAw%3D%3D) to nurture our growing professional community, with a social hour afterward.
events.details:
__Our intention is to:__

- Engage in collective action to develop our Nature-Connected Professionals community with the river as a guide.
- Develop waste-awareness because it’s not just about picking up the trash and shuffling it off to another place.
- Build self-efficacy with small actions that make a difference.
- We’ve partnered with Fresh Water and their Adopt a River toolkit. Supplies provided include gloves, bags, buckets, grabbers, and safety vests.

__Arrive prepared:__ Wear long pants, rubber boots/appropriate footwear and bring gloves if you have them. Also consider sun protection, snacks, and water.

__Accessibility:__ Limited parking nearby, accessible by bus. Trails and restrooms located within walking distance.
events.specialInfo: 🎉 Social gathering follows at [Pryes Brewing Company](https://www.pryesbrewing.com/) until 6:30 PM
